mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0% within a single quarter, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above 5.0% in a monthly reading
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in real economic activity and credit-sensitive demand, which compresses equity risk premiums and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint decline signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 55% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid policy tightening, rising short-term rates amplify the negative impact of contracting real GDP growth on corporate profit margins, producing strongly negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25% in a single month
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or neutral when both rates rise and GDP contracts, or the conditional Sharpe ratio of the strategy falls below 0.10 over a 5-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real personal consumption expenditures (proxied by RSAFS) reflects robust household spending power, which directly supports corporate revenue forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of observations, or the rolling 1-year Rank IC fails to exceed 0.05 in any market regime.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below 110.0, it indicates severe capacity underutilization and weak manufacturing demand, which triggers a repricing of cyclical equities and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite sustained industrial output below the threshold, or the predictive power vanishes (Rank IC < 0.02) after controlling for VIX levels.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising unemployment and elevated federal funds rates creates a dual squeeze on household disposable income and corporate borrowing costs, forcing a sharp downward revision in equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 55% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion where housing starts exceed 1500.0 units, rising construction activity amplifies the positive impact of strong GDP growth on corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when housing permits remain elevated alongside GDP growth, or the conditional alpha drops below zero over a 3-year walk-forward test.
